patterns.enforce_landed_5consec_then_STALL: PROVEN R22->R26 (5 consec): audit registered-but-unenforced NEAR-ZERO linter -> fix-to-zero -> file enforce = lands within week. R27 STALLED: seenmapbool (60+ sites, non-near-zero) did NOT land — fast pattern requires near-zero. For non-near-zero NEW linters, pivot to PRECISION-FIX angle (single-file, self-consistency) which is independently landable (sg27a1 tolowerequalfold self-compare FP).
patterns.linter_precision_subpattern: NEW linters often ship with precision gaps. sg24a1(two-value ResultOf RESOLVED R25), sg27a1(tolowerequalfold self-compare FP RESOLVED R28 caseConvArg+sameOperand+testdata 26-27), sg26a1(seenmapbool escape-blind, OPEN — NOT single-file-trivial, escape analysis harder, lone holdout). Single-file precision fix = high landability (2/3 landed); escape-analysis fix lags. Check each NEW linter testdata okExamples for uncovered FP idioms.
patterns.enforce_precondition_nolint: enforced linter INVARIANT: all 11 enforced have nolint.BuildLineIndex+HasDirective escape hatch. fmterrorfnoverbs gained nolint when enforced (R26 lacked->R28 has). So adding nolint is an enforce precondition for non-near-zero linters (seenmapbool 60+ escaping sites). tolowerequalfold can enforce via full site-conversion (13 EqualFold) WITHOUT nolint, but nolint is the convention. sg28a2.
patterns.wasm_build_tag: *_wasm.go files carry IMPLICIT GOARCH=wasm via filename (no //go:build needed). Host (linux) golint-custom EXCLUDES them. Partition grep hits by build constraint before claiming 'N enforceable'.
patterns.self_consistency: Linter framework's own analyzers should pass enforced linters. sg24a1 RESOLVED R25.

bash_policy: Sandbox blocks: multi-stmt compounds, mkdir/ls/find-exec/wc/cat/sed outside repo, for-loops, backtick grep (cmd subst), heredoc, git on /tmp. go build blocked (lint via Grep/Read). gh issue/search list --json 'Malformed version:'; gh api search/issues label query null items — open-issue check unreliable, rely on memory+7d expiry. Glob/Read/Write work on /tmp; bash CANNOT write/read /tmp/gh-aw/repo-memory (use Write/Edit; append via Edit on unique tail). /tmp/gh-aw/agent/ bash-writable. safeoutputs `. < file.json`. KEEP MEMORY FILES SMALL — 10KB patch limit; prefer Edit over full Write rewrites.
